steps: Go to https://discord.com/developers/applications and log in with your Discord account
Click 'New Application' in the top-right corner
Enter a name for your application and click 'Create'
Click 'Add Bot' and confirm with 'Yes, do it!'
Under the Token section, click 'Reset Token' and confirm
Copy the bot token immediately — it will not be shown again
Under 'Privileged Gateway Intents', enable any intents your bot needs (e.g. Server Members, Message Content)
To invite the bot to a server: go to OAuth2 > URL Generator, select 'bot' scope and required permissions, copy the generated URL and open it in your browser

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Bot Token
credential_fields.description: Your Discord bot token, sent as 'Authorization: Bot TOKEN'

notes: Free with no rate plan required — bot accounts are free
Token is shown only once; reset it immediately if exposed
Token is sent as 'Authorization: Bot <token>' (note the 'Bot' prefix, not 'Bearer')
Message Content Intent must be explicitly enabled for bots in 100+ servers
Your bot must be invited to a server before it can interact with it
Rate limits: 50 requests per second globally; per-route limits apply

test_endpoint.description: Returns the current bot user information
